name: Frick Financial
description_short: Frick Financial Federal Credit Union is an NCUA-insured credit union based in Uniontown, PA, offering savings accounts, CDs, checking, and loan products to members in Washington, Fayette, and Greene counties.
description_long:
Frick Financial Federal Credit Union was established in 1962 and has grown to approximately $177.38 million in assets. As a federally chartered credit union (Charter #14775), it operates under NCUA insurance and serves a specific geographic membership base in southwestern Pennsylvania. The institution maintains its headquarters in Uniontown, PA, and operates multiple branch locations throughout its service area.

The credit union offers a standard suite of deposit products including savings accounts, money market accounts, and certificates of deposit (CDs) with terms ranging from 12 to 60 months. They also provide checking accounts with interest-bearing options, variable rate IRAs, and various loan products including mortgages, home equity lines of credit, auto loans, and personal loans. Their deposit rates as of April 2026 include savings at 0.25% APY and CDs ranging from 0.70% (12-month) to 1.25% APY (60-month).

As a community-focused credit union, Frick Financial distinguishes itself through its membership eligibility restricted to those who live, work, or worship in Washington, Fayette, and Greene counties. This geographic focus allows for localized service and decision-making. The institution maintains a conservative balance sheet with $69.28 million in loans outstanding and $20.94 million in capital, indicating stable operations and member protection.

Prospective members should note that current deposit rates are significantly below national averages for online financial institutions, and loan rate information was not publicly available on their profile as of April 2026. The membership restriction means this credit union is only accessible to a defined geographic population, limiting its utility for consumers outside their service territory.
services: Money market accounts with tiered rates from 0.40% to 0.50% APY
Multiple physical branch locations for member service
pros: NCUA federally insured deposits providing up to $250,000 protection per account category
Established since 1962 with $177.38 million in assets, demonstrating stability and longevity
Full-service financial institution offering savings, CDs, checking, mortgages, auto loans, HELOCs, and personal loans
Multiple branch locations throughout Washington, Fayette, and Greene counties for in-person service
Variable rate IRA option available with rates starting at 0.50% APY
Money market accounts available with tiered rates up to 0.50% APY for higher balances
60-month CD option providing fixed-rate savings for long-term goals
cons: Savings rate of 0.25% APY is substantially below national average (4.20%), making it uncompetitive for savers
CD rates of 0.70-1.25% APY lag significantly behind online credit union and bank averages (4.00-4.59%)
Membership restricted to those living, working, or worshiping in Washington, Fayette, and Greene counties only
Loan rates not publicly available on their profile, requiring direct contact with the institution for quotes
Checking account interest rate of only 0.10% APY offers minimal earnings on liquid funds
best_for: Residents of Washington, Fayette, and Greene counties in Pennsylvania seeking local credit union banking services
Members who value in-person branch service and community-based financial relationships
Consumers who prioritize NCUA federal insurance and need access to multiple loan products
diagnosis: Frick Financial is best for consumers in its three-county Pennsylvania service area who prioritize local, federally insured banking and relationship-based service over competitive deposit rates. The primary caveat is that deposit rates are non-competitive versus national alternatives, making it unsuitable for rate-focused savers seeking maximum returns on savings and CD products.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
